extraction.one_line_summary: BILBAO Pello won from the breakaway with late attacks, moving from 11th to 5th GC while top GC contenders finished 3+ minutes back.
tactical_patterns: JORGENSON Matteo immediately active at race start indicating QuickStep willing to commit climbers to early breaks on long stages.
CAVAGNA Rémi attacked despite Claremont-Ferrand proximity suggesting locals prioritize break inclusion over saving energy for hometown sprint finish.
ZIMMERMANN Georg counterattacked inside final kilometers forcing BILBAO to follow rather than initiate, revealing break sprint tactics favor reactive positioning.
Top GC teams conceded 3+ minutes to BILBAO moving from 11th to 5th, exposing willingness to ignore non-threatening overall positions.

rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: positive: attacked multiple times in finale after four-year wait for Tour stage win, shows hunger converts to aggressive finishing
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: repeatedly attacked in final kilometers recapturing 2021 Tour form when he finished 4th overall
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: only teammate consistently supporting VINGEGAARD through mountain stages, proving most reliable lieutenant
rider_signals.ZIMMERMANN Georg: neutral: strong enough to counterattack for stage win but lacked finishing speed against BILBAO
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michał: neutral: commentary noted sprint threat if caught back suggests dangerous fast-twitch despite being in early break
rider_signals.NEILANDS Krists: neutral: fourth place finish indicates breakaway staying power without winning acceleration
